management_notes:
Jean-Marc Lulin has served as founder, President, CEO, and Director since June 2003 — a 22-year tenure that provides exceptional continuity and deep institutional knowledge of Quebec's James Bay region. As a professional geologist with 30+ years of international experience, Lulin combines rare technical and executive leadership. His innovative prospect-generator model, which uses proprietary AI and data-analytics to identify exploration targets and attract partner funding from majors, has de-risked the company's exploration budget by attracting co-investment from Agnico Eagle, Centerra Gold, Rio Tinto, and KGHM.

The most notable validation signals are strategic: Agnico Eagle (~11%) and Centerra Gold (~9.9%) both hold investor rights agreements with the company, a dual-major-investor structure that implies genuine conviction in the asset portfolio. The May 2025 C$8.7M private placement, led by Centerra at C$0.60/share with Agnico and CDPQ Sodémex co-investing, demonstrates continued institutional support at a premium to market.

The primary concern is Lulin's personal share ownership of approximately 2.4% — relatively low for a founder-led junior given the scale of his tenure and the strategic importance of maintaining alignment. At a ~C$65M market cap, C$8-10M in estimated cash, and multiple active exploration programs, cash management is the critical operational risk for a management team running a multi-property strategy on a limited budget.
geology_notes:
Azimut's flagship Elmer property hosts the Patwon Gold Zone (James Bay, Quebec), with a NI 43-101 resource (filed January 2024): 311,200 oz indicated at 1.93 g/t Au and 513,900 oz inferred at 1.94 g/t Au, totalling approximately 825,100 oz. The grade is moderate for a Quebec gold project and the deposit style (intrusion-related or greenstone-hosted, open-pit amenable) is well-established in the James Bay belt. A 10,000-metre drill program commenced January 2026 to expand the resource ahead of an updated MRE and planned PEA.

The genuinely exciting emerging catalyst is the Rosa Zone discovery at the Wabamisk property (approximately 250 km from Elmer), where a maiden 3,633-metre, 26-hole program completed late 2025 identified visible gold in 11 of 26 drill holes (42%). Results released February 9, 2026 include 3.26 g/t Au over 9.35 m within 28.90 m at 1.29 g/t Au, with the zone traced over 1.4 km strike and open in all directions. A follow-up 2,000-metre program was active as of March 2026 with assays pending.

The Wabamisk Fortin Zone (antimony-gold) defines a 1.8 km strike, 250 m depth system of antimony mineralization — one of the largest identified in Canada at a time when antimony has been designated a critical mineral. The Wabamisk East lithium pegmatite field (up to 7.43% Li2O in surface samples) adds optionality. Rosa Zone's visible gold discovery is the most significant near-term geological catalyst across the entire portfolio.
capital_notes:
Azimut has approximately 100.7-101 million basic shares outstanding — a tight share structure by TSXV standards. The estimated cash position of C$8-10M is based on the C$8.7M raised in the May 2025 strategic placement and extrapolated for subsequent exploration expenditures; precise cash balance from financial statements was not confirmed in available public sources. The share count is tight and Centerra holds an anti-dilution right to maintain its ~9.9% position in future financings.

The primary capital concern is the multi-property exploration ambition versus the limited treasury. The company is running simultaneous programs at Elmer/Patwon (10,000m drill), Wabamisk Rosa Zone (2,000m follow-up), and evaluating Wabamisk East lithium — a three-front exploration budget on estimated C$8-10M in cash. The prospect-generator model helps by attracting partner-funded drilling, but Azimut's 100%-owned properties (Elmer, Wabamisk) require direct expenditure.

No debt. Annual net burn rate is modest for a company of this size, and stock options issued December 2025 align the team with shareholders. Cash runway is estimated at 12-18 months on existing treasury, necessitating a financing in late 2026 or early 2027 if the Rosa Zone discovery requires an aggressive follow-up campaign.
catalyst_notes:
Multiple near-term catalysts are active simultaneously, which is unusual for a ~C$65M market cap company and reflects the multi-property strategy. Most immediate: follow-up Rosa Zone drill assay results from the active 2,000-metre program at Wabamisk (Q2 2026) — visible gold in 42% of maiden program holes is an exceptional discovery metric that the market has only partially priced in. Second: ongoing results from the 10,000-metre Elmer/Patwon drill program (H1 2026), targeting resource expansion ahead of an updated MRE.

Longer-dated catalysts include: updated NI 43-101 MRE for Patwon (H2 2026 or early 2027 depending on drill results), a PEA for Elmer (post-updated MRE, potentially 2027), and any strategic partnership expansion or formal M&A approach from Agnico Eagle or Centerra. The Wabamisk East lithium pegmatite program (up to 7.43% Li2O surface samples) is in early planning stages and adds a separate commodity optionality.

The Rosa Zone discovery is the defining catalyst in the 12-month horizon. Comparable discovery trajectories in the James Bay region (e.g., James Bay Gold, Patriot Battery Metals) demonstrate that visible gold in maiden programs can reprice small explorers dramatically on follow-up results.
acquisition_notes:
Azimut trades at an enterprise value of approximately C$55-57M, implying approximately USD$49/oz in-ground on the 825,100 oz Elmer/Patwon resource alone — with no value assigned to Rosa Zone (no resource), Wabamisk antimony, or Wabamisk East lithium. This is inexpensive for a Quebec gold explorer with two strategic investors holding investor rights agreements, though the moderate grade (1.93-1.94 g/t Au) and pre-PEA stage appropriately limit the multiple.

Agnico Eagle's ~11% stake in a James Bay explorer is not a passive financial investment — Agnico operates three mills in the Val-d'Or region and has been the dominant consolidator in Quebec gold for a decade (Détour, Meliadine, Canadian Malartic). Centerra's ~9.9% stake similarly reflects strategic rather than passive interest. The dual-major-investor structure with investor rights agreements creates a structured option for either company to acquire the asset as the resource matures.

The Rosa Zone discovery represents potentially untapped embedded optionality: a high-grade gold discovery in James Bay with visible gold in 42% of maiden holes is the type of early-stage signal that catalysed multi-hundred-million-dollar re-ratings in comparable exploration stories. If follow-up drilling confirms a meaningful high-grade zone, the acquisition value calculus shifts significantly.
analyst_summary:
WATCH (17/25). Azimut is the most speculative of the three Quebec explorers in this batch — a high-optionality story built on a moderate-grade primary resource (825,100 oz at 1.93 g/t Au at Elmer/Patwon), a potentially transformative Rosa Zone gold discovery (visible gold in 42% of maiden holes), and two credible strategic investors in Agnico Eagle and Centerra Gold. The multi-property model creates diversified catalysts but also stretches a limited treasury across simultaneous exploration programs. Score reflects the genuine uncertainty that comes with a pre-PEA, pre-updated-MRE position.

The primary risk is cash management: C$8-10M against multiple active drill programs and an ambitious 2026 exploration agenda is tight. If Rosa Zone follow-up results disappoint or require a major financing at a discount, downside is material. The low personal insider ownership by founder Lulin (~2.4%) and the absence of a resource at Rosa Zone or Wabamisk limit near-term valuation floor.

The key catalyst to watch is the Rosa Zone follow-up drill assay results expected Q2 2026. This is a binary event: if the 2,000-metre follow-up confirms high-grade gold over economic widths along the 1.4 km known strike, the discovery re-rating could move Azimut to BUY territory and likely attract a major financing round. If results disappoint, the stock could retest prior lows. Watch for these results before committing capital.
